title: rubus arrhenii subsp. chamaearrhenii
intro: Rubus arrhenii subsp. chamaearrhenii, also known as the Alpine Bramble or Chamaearrhenius, is a lesser-known subspecies of the Rubus arrhenii plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and characterized by its compact growth habit and small, white or pink flowers. The Alpine Bramble has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Rubus arrhenii subsp. chamaearrhenii are attributed to its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which provide numerous health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or tincture, and it can also be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
